prosody.intonation: flat pitch contours, narrow pitch range typical of L1 transfer
prosody.fluencyNotes: Generally fluent but lacks connected speech features (linking, assimilation); words are often produced in isolation with hard glottal onsets on initial vowels.

strengths: Clear articulation of complex consonant clusters
Consistent placement of primary lexical stress

recommendedFocus.reason: High functional load; substitution alters word meaning and creates noticeable foreignness.
Reducing hard glottal attacks on initial vowels will improve rhythm and naturalness.
Final obstruent devoicing changes grammatical markers and word meanings.

result.transcript: Yesterday, the weather was really beautiful. So I decided to go for a walk through the park near my house. The trees were green and the birds were singing everywhere. I think spring's my favorite time of the year. I walked along the river and watched some children playing with a big red ball. They were laughing and having so much fun together. One little girl threw the ball very high and it landed right in the water. Her father had to reach in and pull it out. After that, I stopped at a small village shop to buy some bread and a bottle of juice.
